mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses corporate wage costs and signals economic weakness, prompting equity market declines over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power during recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates a broad-based collapse in aggregate demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when either series declines independently, or the interaction term yields a negative out-of-sample R-squared.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production signal immediate supply chain disruptions and earnings downgrades, causing rapid negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day forward returns during supply shocks, or the predictive accuracy drops below 45 percent in post-shock environments.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values erode real corporate margins and trigger expectations of tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of high-inflation observations, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL changes and 5-day returns is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP falls below 19500 billion chained 2017 dollars, severe output contraction forces corporate earnings revisions downward, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The threshold signal fails to predict negative returns when GDPC1 drops below 19500, or the out-of-sample hit rate falls below 40 percent in recessionary quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total nonfarm payrolls decline while the unemployment rate rises simultaneously confirms structural labor market deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models, or forward returns remain positive when both series move in the specified direction.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate environment, rising industrial production signals robust manufacturing demand and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during easing cycles, or the predictive accuracy for positive returns drops below 45 percent when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0 percent.
